explicit_text: My name is Yu-Lin Amy Lee, and I work in primary care, specializing in internal medicine and pediatrics. I completed my medical training in Taiwan and later did a fellowship in hospice and palliative care. Over the past year, especially during the COVID-19 pandemic, I've become more vocal about racial issues and healthcare disparities, feeling it's important to represent Asian-Americans and speak up. Wearing my full name on my white coat is a symbol of pride after years of struggling with identity and assimilation.
implicit_text: My name is Yu-Lin Amy Lee, and I work in primary care, specializing in internal medicine and pediatrics. I completed my medical training in an East Asian country and later did a fellowship in hospice and palliative care. Over the past year, especially during the recent global health crisis, I've become more vocal about racial issues and healthcare disparities, feeling it's important to represent my ethnic community and speak up. Wearing my full name on my white coat is a symbol of pride after years of struggling with identity and assimilation.

explicit_text: I did my medical school training in Taiwan, which shaped much of my approach to patient care. There, we often considered patients' faith and social backgrounds more deeply, which influenced how I now engage with my patients in the U.S. My experience in Taiwan gave me a foundation that I have built upon, especially during the challenges of the pandemic and racial reckoning in America.
implicit_text: I did my medical school training in an island nation in East Asia, which shaped much of my approach to patient care. There, we often considered patients' faith and social backgrounds more deeply, which influenced how I now engage with my patients in the U.S. My experience in that country gave me a foundation that I have built upon, especially during the challenges of the pandemic and racial reckoning in America.

explicit_text: The COVID-19 pandemic has been a long and difficult season for healthcare, bringing many challenges and changes almost overnight. It exposed and amplified existing healthcare disparities, especially among Black and Brown populations, which we had to confront head-on. As a frontline worker, I had to adapt quickly with my care team to new screening and outreach methods, while also facing personal attacks of racism linked to the virus.
implicit_text: The recent global health crisis has been a long and difficult season for healthcare, bringing many challenges and changes almost overnight. It exposed and amplified existing healthcare disparities, especially among Black and Brown populations, which we had to confront head-on. As a frontline worker, I had to adapt quickly with my care team to new screening and outreach methods, while also facing personal attacks of racism linked to the virus.

explicit_text: The Black Lives Matter movement deeply influenced how I connected with my patients during the past year. Our clinic openly supported the movement by attending rallies, wearing ally pins, and having honest conversations about racial injustice. This helped build stronger therapeutic relationships, especially with Black patients and families, showing them that I am not just their doctor but also an ally who cares about their social and emotional wellbeing.
implicit_text: The movement advocating for racial justice and equality deeply influenced how I connected with my patients during the past year. Our clinic openly supported the cause by attending rallies, wearing ally pins, and having honest conversations about racial injustice. This helped build stronger therapeutic relationships, especially with Black patients and families, showing them that I am not just their doctor but also an ally who cares about their social and emotional wellbeing.

entity: Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act
explicit_text: While I always respect patient privacy and confidentiality under the Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act, I have found ways to share meaningful stories that illustrate the social and emotional challenges my patients face. HIPAA guides how I protect their information, but it doesn't prevent me from addressing broader issues like racism and mental health in a safe and supportive environment. This balance is crucial in providing holistic care.
implicit_text: While I always respect patient privacy and confidentiality under federal health information protection laws, I have found ways to share meaningful stories that illustrate the social and emotional challenges my patients face. These regulations guide how I protect their information, but they don't prevent me from addressing broader issues like racism and mental health in a safe and supportive environment. This balance is crucial in providing holistic care.

explicit_text: As an Asian-American, I have often felt invisible or trivialized, especially during the recent rise in anti-Asian attacks linked to the pandemic. It has been painful to experience xenophobia firsthand, from being called the coronavirus to facing stereotypes and microaggressions throughout my life. However, I have grown to embrace my identity proudly, wearing my full name and sharing my culture openly, hoping to give voice to the Asian-American community and encourage others to speak up.
implicit_text: As a member of a minority ethnic group from East Asia, I have often felt invisible or trivialized, especially during the recent rise in attacks linked to the global health crisis. It has been painful to experience xenophobia firsthand, from being called the virus to facing stereotypes and microaggressions throughout my life. However, I have grown to embrace my identity proudly, wearing my full name and sharing my culture openly, hoping to give voice to my community and encourage others to speak up.
